articles.title: APSDMA warns of heatwave, thunderstorms over next two days - The New Indian Express
articles.full_text:
Representative Image Photo | Express
VIJAYAWADA: The AP State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) has issued weather advisory, warning of extreme heat conditions and thunderstorm activity across several districts over the next two days.
Managing Director Prakhar Jain said the State is experiencing varied weather patterns, with some regions recording severe heat while others are likely to witness sudden rains accompanied by lightning.
For Thursday, APSDMA forecasts severe heatwave conditions in 10 mandals including Bobbili, Bondapalli, and Ramabhadrapuram in Vizianagaram; Gurugubilli, Makkuva, Pachipenta, Manyam, Salur, and Seethanagaram in Manyam; and Ananthagiri in Alluri district. Another 20 mandals are expected to face heatwave.
At the same time, a trough is likely to trigger light to moderate rains with thunderstorms in Visakhapatnam and Anakapalli districts. Scattered thundershowers are expected in North Andhra, Anantapur, Sri Sathya Sai, Kadapa, and Annamayya districts.
The APSDMA has urged farmers not to take shelter under trees during thunderstorms and advised the public to remain vigilant. The authority stressed that precautionary measures are essential until weather conditions stabilise.

articles.full_text:
- Stay safe during extreme heatwave, follow advisory: CM Bhajan Lal Sharma
Trending
Jaipur: CM Bhajan Lal Sharma urged people to take precautions amid the ongoing severe heatwave across Rajasthan and to strictly follow the state govt’s advisory.“Citizens should ensure all necessary precautions to protect themselves from the adverse effects of extreme heat,” the chief minister said, advising children, elderly persons, pregnant women and those with illnesses to take extra care.He urged people to avoid stepping out unnecessarily during peak hours. “People should avoid going out in the sun between 12 noon and 3 pm unless absolutely necessary,” he said.Highlighting the risks of heatstroke, Sharma added, “In case of serious symptoms such as unconsciousness or high fever, immediate medical attention should be sought.” He said people can visit the nearest health centre or contact emergency services on 108 or 112 for timely assistance.The chief minister also appealed to workers, farmers and livestock rearers to remain cautious. “Those working outdoors must take special precautions to prevent heatstroke,” he said.Urging compassion, he added, “People should also ensure that animals and birds have access to food and water during this extreme weather.”Urging compassion, he added, “People should also ensure that animals and birds have access to food and water during this extreme weather.”

articles.full_text:
3 Min Read
India is expected to witness a mix of heatwave conditions and widespread rainfall activity from April 30 onwards, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasting sharp contrasts across regions.
From April 30, fairly widespread to widespread rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds is likely over Northeast India for the next five days, the IMD said in its weather bulletin.
Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim are also set to receive heavy rainfall between April 30 and May 3, with isolated very heavy showers likely on April 30 and May 1, the IMD said.
In eastern India, light to moderate rainfall with thunderstorms and gusty winds is likely over Bihar till May 3, and over Jharkhand and Odisha till May 5.

The IMD has forecast scattered rainfall and thunderstorm activity over parts of Northwest India, including Punjab, Haryana, Delhi and Rajasthan, between May 2 and 5.
Uttarakhand is likely to experience continued rainfall and thunderstorm activity till May 5.
Central India, including Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh, is expected to receive isolated rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms and gusty winds over the next five days.
South India to see continued showers
Southern states, including Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Telangana and Karnataka, are likely to witness scattered rainfall with thunderstorms and gusty winds till May 3.
Heavy rainfall is also likely over Kerala and Mahe till May 2, and over Tamil Nadu and adjoining regions between April 30 and May 2.

Despite the rainfall activity, heatwave conditions are likely to prevail in isolated pockets of Vidarbha and West Rajasthan between May 1 and 3, the IMD said.
Hot and humid weather is expected to continue over parts of Odisha, coastal Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu till May 3.
The IMD also indicated a gradual fall in maximum temperatures by 2-5 degrees Celsius across several regions, including Uttar Pradesh, East India and parts of Central India, till early May, followed by slight fluctuations.
Delhi to get brief relief, temperatures to rise later
In Delhi-NCR, April 30 is likely to bring partly cloudy skies with light rain, thunderstorms and gusty winds reaching up to 50 kmph in the afternoon.
Day temperatures are expected to range between 35-37 degrees Celsius, remaining below normal.

However, temperatures are likely to rise again from May 1, with maximum temperatures touching 38-41 degrees Celsius by May 2 under partly cloudy conditions.
Fishermen advised caution
The IMD has also advised fishermen to avoid venturing into the Bay of Bengal, particularly along the West Bengal and Odisha coasts, between April 30 and May 4 due to rough sea conditions.
From April 30, fairly widespread to widespread rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds is likely over Northeast India for the next five days, the IMD said in its weather bulletin.
Heavy to very heavy rainfall is expected over Arunachal Pradesh between April 30 and May 2, and over Assam and Meghalaya till May 3.
Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim are also set to receive heavy rainfall between April 30 and May 3, with isolated very heavy showers likely on April 30 and May 1, the IMD said.
In eastern India, light to moderate rainfall with thunderstorms and gusty winds is likely over Bihar till May 3, and over Jharkhand and Odisha till May 5.

However, temperatures are likely to rise again from May 1, with maximum temperatures touching 38-41 degrees Celsius by May 2 under partly cloudy conditions.
Fishermen advised caution
The IMD has also advised fishermen to avoid venturing into the Bay of Bengal, particularly along the West Bengal and Odisha coasts, between April 30 and May 4 due to rough sea conditions.

articles.title: Heatwave in North and Central India; Rainfall in Northeast Over Next Few Days: IMD - News On AIR
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast heatwave conditions over northwest and central parts of the country during next three days and abate thereafter. According to IMD, Jammu-Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh and Delhi will experience heat waves conditions till tomorrow.
The weather agency has predicted Hot and humid weather conditions in isolated pockets over Odisha tomorrow and Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal till wednesday. It also said that Punjab, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Chandigarh Delhi, Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh will likely experience warm night conditions at isolated pockets.
The met department has predicted heavy to very heavy rainfall at isolated places over Arunachal Pradesh, Assam and Meghalaya tomorrow. The weather body predicted thunderstorm activity over East India during next one week.
